title: DataPipe Pipeline Config
description: Pipeline configuration for DataPipe CLI — defines source, mapping, and target.

$schema.description: Path to the JSON Schema (for IDE autocomplete & validation)

name.description: Name of the pipeline, displayed in the CLI header

mapping.description: List of mapping rules from source fields to target database columns

JsonSource.description: Read data from a local JSON file

filePath.description: Path to the JSON file (relative or absolute)

resultPath.description: Dot-path to the target data array inside the JSON file, e.g. "data.users"

CsvSource.description: Read data from a local CSV file

filePath.description: Path to the CSV file (relative or absolute)

delimiter.description: Delimiter separating columns in the CSV

hasHeader.description: Whether the first row contains column headers

ApiSource.description: Fetch data from one or more REST API endpoints

requests.description: List of API requests to be performed in each page iteration

mergeKey.description: Key attribute used to merge items from multiple requests

delayMs.description: Rate limiting delay between API requests in milliseconds

ApiRequest.description: A single API endpoint request configuration

id.description: Unique key identifier for the response in merged results, e.g. "main_api"

url.description: Request URL. Use {param} placeholder for pagination indexes, e.g. "https://api.com/items/{id}"

resultPath.description: Dot-path to extract target array data from JSON response, e.g. "data.items"

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers, e.g. Authorization keys

PaginationConfig.description: Pagination configuration for the API source

RangePagination.description: Loop pagination from a starting index to an ending index

param.description: URL query or template placeholder name to replace, e.g. "id" to replace {id}

CursorPagination.description: Cursor-based token pagination, loops until the next cursor property is null

param.description: Query query param name for cursor token, e.g. "cursor" or "after"

nextPath.description: Dot-path to extract the next page token from responses, e.g. "meta.next_cursor"

PostgresTarget.description: Write data to a PostgreSQL database

SqliteTarget.description: Write data to a SQLite database

filePath.description: Local path to the SQLite database file

OperationConfig.description: Mode of writing entries to database tables

mode.description: insert = normal insert (fails on duplicate) | upsert = insert or update on conflict | update = update existing rows using matching condition

conflictOn.description: Column names triggering ON CONFLICT upsert updates (required for upsert mode)

updateWhere.description: Primary key / column matching attributes for database updates (required for update mode)

UpdateWhereClause.description: Column matching configuration for update operations

column.description: Target database table column name used for matching

fromField.description: Source item field name providing the value to match

TransformKey.description: toInt = parse as integer | toFloat = parse as float | toString = convert to string | toJsonString = stringify object to JSON string | toISODate = parse to ISO 8601 string | toLower = lowercase string | toUpper = uppercase string | trim = trim outer whitespace | nullIfEmpty = return null if field is empty or blank

MappingRule.description: Rule defining mapping from a source field to a target table column

from.description: Dot-path identifier to extract from the source item, e.g. "user.profile.id" or "SKU"

to.description: Target column name in the database table

default.description: Fallback value if source field extracts to null or undefined

expand.description: When true, this field contains a nested array which is expanded/flattened into multiple rows

mapping.description: Nested mapping rules applied to child elements (required when expand is true)
